queued.title: Rebuild cutover + legacy retirement
queued.what: Finish the remaining cutover work that keeps the rebuild from running as the sole equities runtime. Current remaining seams: remove rebuild startup dependence on legacy premarket artifacts where still present, finish stewardship beyond EXIT (ADD/REDUCE), finish the governed operator actions on the app, and retire the last legacy-assist scheduling/execution seams only after the rebuild paths are proven. Checkpoint-05 evidence accumulation continues in parallel and is not the only blocker for finishing the architecture.
queued.prereq: Existing rebuild core, strategy bank, paper decision-support path, and checkpoint-05 runner already landed.

queued.title: Capital allocation execution — park idle capital in BIL/SGOV, unpark for trades
queued.what: Extend the capital allocation scaffold (config/rebuild_capital_allocation.json + capital_allocation.py service) into an execution path. When the active equities sleeve has no trades and capital is sitting idle, produce a parking order (BUY BIL/SGOV for the cash management reserve portion). When the sleeve produces a real trade plan, produce a corresponding unpark order (SELL BIL/SGOV to free capital). Both flow through the approval queue in DECISION_SUPPORT or auto-execute in AUTONOMOUS_PAPER. Requires: (1) allocation service reads current positions + allocation config + trade plan status, (2) produces parking/unparking actions in portfolio_action_plan.json, (3) bridge handles BIL/SGOV orders alongside strategy orders, (4) stewardship treats parked positions as CASH_MANAGEMENT origin (not strategy-owned, no stop/target rules). The config already defines reserve_vehicle_candidates, sleeve targets, and parking_policy per sleeve — this ticket wires the execution.
queued.blocker: Codex must land AUTONOMOUS_PAPER bridge support (Q-084 blocker 1) first since parking orders need the same execution path
queued.prereq: Capital allocation config landed (done 2026-04-13). AUTONOMOUS_PAPER bridge support (Q-084 blocker 1). Stewardship v1 (done).

queued.title: Vercel approval buttons — long-term approval channel on trading page
queued.what: Add approve/reject buttons to the Vercel trading page for Decision Support mode. Same control-plane write path as Telegram (approval_queue.json + approval_events.jsonl). Discord remains the broadcast and incidents surface; Vercel should stay focused on review and operator action, not become a separate backend. Requires: (1) app visual refactor complete (current pass), (2) Q-073 proven so control-plane artifacts are battle-tested, (3) audited request path per doc 16 (UI requests -> control plane validates -> apply/deny). Display: pending approval items with expiry, summary, approve/reject action, required operator note on reject.
queued.blocker: Q-073 proven, app visual refactor complete
queued.prereq: Q-073 Telegram approval transport working. Trading page visual refactor complete. Doc 16 audited request path wired.

queued.title: Autonomous overnight backtesting — OpenClaw cron sweep layer
queued.what:
After Q-076B produces manually-tested strategy skeletons, wire an autonomous overnight sweep layer. NARROWED SCOPE per Codex feedback: first deliverable is disk-only nightly sweep orchestration. Operator-feed/dashboard leaderboard surfaces come AFTER the strategy registry and result schema stabilize.

FIRST DELIVERABLE (disk-only):
- OpenClaw cron job runs nightly (e.g. 01:00 ET)
- Picks next queued sweep from a manifest file
- Runs the validation runtime campaign (parameter sweep x time windows)
- Writes results to backtest/campaigns/<campaign_id>/
- Writes a cross-strategy leaderboard to state/rebuild_reports/strategy_leaderboard.json
- Reports summary to Telegram or claude-inbox.md

LATER DELIVERABLE (after schema settles):
- strategy_leaderboard.json added to operator feed contract
- Trading page gets a Strategy Research section (Claude/Opus scope)
- Dashboard shows tested families, verdict distribution, top performers

GUARDRAILS:
- Maximum nightly compute budget (e.g. 500 runs/night)
- All results on disk with full reproducibility
- No parameter combination tested without a parent hypothesis from Q-076B
- Leaderboard includes negative results (FAILs visible, not just PASSes)
- Does NOT generate hypotheses autonomously
- Does NOT use LLMs inside the backtest loop
queued.blocker: Q-076 must produce at least 2-3 manually validated strategy skeletons first
queued.prereq: Q-076A + Q-076B complete (generalized runtime, at least 2 skeletons tested manually). Strategy registry pattern established. Result schema stable.

queued.title: Broad-market defensive sleeve research — revive defensive_value_proxy + new strategy families
queued.what: The equities momentum sleeve (regime_aware_momentum) is proven to work in tech leadership but actively degrades on broad-market names. Broad-market exposure needs a DIFFERENT strategy family, not a diluted momentum strategy. This ticket revives defensive_value_proxy.py with a proper 20-30 name diversified universe, runs it through the Q-083 multi-era framework, and evaluates whether it can serve as a stability counterweight to the tech momentum sleeve. Also scope whether a new strategy family is needed (e.g. quality/low-vol, mean-reversion, or value-tilt entry logic designed for non-tech names). The end state is two sub-sleeves within equities: tech momentum (opportunity/growth) + broad defensive (stability/floor). Capital split between sub-sleeves could be regime-informed (more to tech in CALM, more to defensive in VOLATILE). Q-076B showed defensive_value_proxy had interesting inconclusive variants with positive DM excess and low drawdowns, but sample-starved. Needs broader universe + more eras to get a real read.
queued.prereq: Q-085 Tier 2 results in (don't preempt dynamic tech screening). Q-083 multi-era framework operational (done). defensive_value_proxy.py skeleton exists (done from Q-076B).

queued.title: Governed strategy-select + mode toggle UI — operator actions on trading page
queued.what: Add operator-action UI to the trading page: (1) a strategy-select control on the Promoted Strategy card letting the operator pick any banked record as the active strategy, and (2) a mode toggle letting the operator request SHADOW / AUTONOMOUS_PAPER / DECISION_SUPPORT transitions. Both must route through a governed request contract, NOT a direct file write. Flow: UI posts an intent -> control plane validates -> writes to approval_queue.json + events log (same control plane as trade approvals per doc 16) -> backend applies (calls bin/strategy_bank.py select --actor <user> or mode-transition handler) -> feed reflects new active record / mode on next push. Depends on Codex landing the governed request path for strategy selection and mode transitions; until then the card stays display-only. Sequenced alongside Q-075 (Vercel approval buttons) because both ride the same request/approval pipe.
queued.blocker: Codex must define the governed request contract for strategy-select + mode-transition intents (doc 16 extension). Q-073 Telegram transport proven reduces risk on the shared pipe.
queued.prereq: Promoted Strategy display card shipped (this session). Doc 16 request contract extended to cover strategy_bank.select and mode_transition intents. Q-073 control plane proven. push-operator-feed.py surfaces request-status fields so the UI can show pending/applied/failed states.

queued.title: Sleeve-segregated operator feed + dashboard — stocks / options / crypto / predictions
queued.what: Design and stage the transition from a single implicit-stocks strategy bank to per-sleeve strategy banks. Sleeves are asset-class containers: stocks, options, crypto, prediction markets. Each sleeve owns its own strategy bank, validation runtime entry points, market-data provider wiring, risk envelope, and promotion criteria. Sub-sleeves live inside each sleeve (e.g. options -> covered-calls / protective-puts / directional-spreads; stocks -> momentum / mean-reversion / defensive). The bench runtime should accept a sleeve descriptor so the same registry/runner can host every asset class without forking. Operator feed v2 shape (proposed): operator.sleeves = { stocks: {strategy_bank, active_record_id, positions_slice, approval_slice}, options: {...}, crypto: {...}, predictions: {...} }. Dashboard becomes a sleeve switcher (segmented control at top of trading page) with each sleeve rendering its own Promoted Strategy card, positions list, approval queue, and bank. Deliverables: (1) Codex-owned design doc proposing the sleeve-shaped contract v2 and the bench-runtime generalization (what stays shared, what forks per sleeve), (2) migration plan keeping contract v1 consumers alive until cutover, (3) Claude-owned UI mockup/plan for the sleeve switcher and per-sleeve page composition, (4) identification of first non-stocks sleeve to stand up end-to-end (likely options given Q-044 Phase 1 path already exists).
queued.blocker: Needs design alignment before build. Do not start implementation until sleeve contract v2 is written and reviewed.
queued.prereq: Q-078 governed request contract in flight (so sleeve switcher + per-sleeve actions ride the same governed pipe). Q-076B momentum bank stable as the reference sleeve. Q-044 options execution path sufficient to model a second sleeve.

queued.title: Auto-register PASS variants + auto-push operator feed on bank change
queued.what: Close the two manual gaps between 'bench produces a PASS variant' and 'dashboard shows it'. (1) Add `--auto-register-pass` flag to `bin/run_validation_campaign.py` so that when a campaign finishes with any PASS variant, the runner automatically calls `bin/strategy_bank.py register-from-campaign` for each passing variant. Must NOT auto-activate — selection stays governed through Q-078's intent contract when that lands. Current default stays manual for safety during research phase; flag is opt-in per campaign. (2) File-change trigger on `state/rebuild_latest/strategy_bank.json` (and `active_strategy.json`) that runs `bash claw-dashboard/scripts/push-operator-feed.sh`. Either a lightweight inotify/watchman daemon, a post-write hook inside `bin/strategy_bank.py`, or a cron every N minutes that checks file mtime — Codex's call. Goal: dashboard reflects new bank state within minutes of promotion without manual push.
queued.prereq: None — current strategy bank + push-operator-feed pipeline already works end-to-end manually. Q-078 is not a prereq because auto-activation is explicitly out of scope here.

queued.title: Options strategy expansion — execution path + directional plays
queued.what: AMD put (+$275, 39.6%) outperformed all 13 equity positions combined. Options are currently hedge-only (protective put screener). No execution path exists. Build: (1) options order submitter for Alpaca paper, (2) covered call screener for income in CALM regimes, (3) directional options plays (call/put spreads) as strategy agent alternative to equity entries. Regime detection (HMM + VIX + pre-market macro) is more useful for options than equities — directly informs whether options are cheap or expensive.
queued.prereq: Verify Alpaca paper supports options orders. Protective put screener already producing candidates.

queued.title: Dashboard cleanup — kill stale tabs, surface stewardship on Trading
queued.what: Remove Tunables and Pipeline tabs (dead legacy surfaces). Surface stewardship data on the Trading tab once the operator feed exposes it (position book summary, review recommendations, action plan status). This is the focused dashboard TLC pass — no sleeve switcher, no new tabs, just clean up what's dead and add the one high-value missing section. Sleeve switcher (Q-079) comes later when there are two sleeves to switch between.
queued.blocker: Stewardship section in operator feed (Codex needs to add stewardship_summary to push-operator-feed.py). Tab removal is unblocked.
queued.prereq: Tab removal: none, can do immediately. Stewardship surfacing: operator feed needs stewardship_summary section.

queued.title: Crypto sub-sleeve v1 — BTC 4h feasibility spike
queued.what: First validation spike for a BTC-only 4h momentum/breakout strategy on the existing bench. Research only — no execution, no state changes, no crypto infrastructure. Extends validation data model/provider to support timestamped intraday bars and crypto research path (current runtime is daily/equity-shaped). Builds CryptoBTCDataSource (yfinance 1h resampled to 4h, UTC-anchored), defines 24/7 replay calendar for 4h bars. Frozen dataset required before any validation run. Walk-forward: 90-calendar-day train / 30-calendar-day test folds over 4h bars. Comparison baseline: BTC buy-and-hold + no-trade control. Fee assumption: 0.2% round-trip. Gate criteria: Sharpe >1.0, max DD <25%, PF >1.5, min 50 trades, survives 0.3% slippage sweep. If FAIL: closes this BTC 4h hypothesis, not the entire crypto sleeve.
queued.prereq: Existing bench stack (Q-076A/B complete). Validation runtime generalized. Strategy registry pattern established.

queued.title: Crypto data upgrade — longer-history BTC for multi-era validation
queued.what: If Q-090a shows signal, upgrade BTC data source for proper multi-cycle coverage (2022 bear, 2023 recovery, 2024 bull, 2025 consolidation). Options: paid data API, daily-bar regime characterization, or ccxt for exchange-native historical OHLCV. Re-run validation with multi-era framework across named crypto eras.
queued.blocker: Q-090a must show positive signal worth pursuing

queued.title: Multi-user product foundation — scope discipline for consumer-app readiness
queued.what: Guardrail ticket establishing scope discipline so future slices avoid single-user corners. Doctrine: portfolio_id is the unit of isolation (not user), actor identity is separate from state ownership, stop hardcoding rebuild_latest in domain logic, prefer request-service-artifact patterns over CLI-only wrappers, pass scope/actor explicitly when cheap, treat sleeves as scoped product surfaces. Does not change what we build next but changes how we build it. Tightened per Codex review 2026-04-15.

queued.title: Governed manual trade actions — operator buy/sell/close path
queued.what: Add a clean operator-initiated manual trade path so Jacob can explicitly say buy this, sell that, or close this position without going around the rebuild. Routes through the same governed execution stack as normal plans, creates a durable audit trail, and keeps manual actions legible inside the state artifacts.
queued.prereq: Existing execution bridge / approval queue / cash-management execution scaffolding already landed.

completed.title: Legacy pipeline cutover — kill legacy, run rebuild-only
completed.what: Weekend cutover to rebuild-only operation. Three blockers: (1) AUTONOMOUS_PAPER bridge support for both entry and EXIT bridges, (2) minimal rebuild-native incident alerting (premarket failed, midday stalled, action plan pending, bridge failed), (3) intraday protection decision (keep legacy broker_reconciler vs build rebuild watcher). Plus: final legacy snapshot, rollback script, disable 20 OpenClaw crons + 10 system crontab entries, verification checklist by time window. Full checklist at memory/q084-legacy-cutover-checklist.md.

completed.prereq: At least 1 full rebuild trading day in SHADOW/DECISION_SUPPORT (2026-04-13 done). Operator preflight CLI working.

completed.title: Queue auto-close mechanism - one-time scrub done, automation still needed

The remaining work is the reliable auto-close path so the queue stays honest without manual cleanup. Candidates remain: ticket frontmatter status, commit trailers (Closes: Q-NNN), or a lightweight queue-close CLI/nightly reconciler.

completed.title: OpenClaw 2026.4.5 upgrade — cron auto-replay, prompt cache improvements, Claude CLI hardening
completed.what: Upgrade to OpenClaw 2026.4.5. Adopts: cron replay interrupted recurring jobs on gateway restart (#60583, fixes 2026-04-06 power-outage manual recovery gap), cron failure notifications via primary delivery channel (#60622), prompt caching improvements (stable system prompt fingerprints, removed duplicate tool inventories, cache reuse in status --verbose — direct cost reduction given April pricing change), Claude CLI security hardening + dedicated health checks, heartbeat skip-wake when session lane busy, live model switching fix, auto-reply lifecycle fix on gateway restart. Breaking: legacy public config aliases removed (talk.voiceId, sandbox.perSession, channel allow toggles — doctor --fix migrates), Anthropic Claude CLI backend removed from new onboarding (existing profiles runnable), bundled CLI text-provider backends removed. Plan: Saturday morning backup → upgrade → doctor --fix → verify Claude CLI auth → single job test → Saturday afternoon pipeline-validate.sh dry run → Sunday nightly validator → Monday live. NOT part of audit-and-rebuild freeze — infrastructure track, separate from Q-068/069/070.
completed.prereq: Saturday window (markets closed). Backup of ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json and cron list JSON before upgrade.
completed.close_note: OpenClaw 2026.4.5 upgrade completed last week by Jacob

completed.title: Rebuild Obsidian vault docs against the new rebuild architecture
completed.what: Step 1 vault audit is already done and staging exists. Remaining work: apply the rebuild-era architecture/docs refresh to the real vault through the staging/sync path, keep the daily reports and technical architecture notes current, and finish retiring legacy-era vault pages that still describe the old 16-agent system as current.
completed.blocker: Q-072 closed provides the new strategic frame. Rebuild architecture docs (08/12/13/16/17/19) already exist on disk. Not blocked — can start whenever Jacob has bandwidth.
completed.prereq: Vault audit complete; staging/sync pattern established; remaining work is curated apply + ongoing maintenance.
completed.close_note: Obsidian vault rebuild complete. 9 architecture notes, rewritten README/Quick Reference/Glossary, new daily template, legacy notes archived. Applied to Windows vault.

completed.title: Slippage sensitivity sweep + turnover cost reporting for campaign evaluator
completed.what: Add slippage sensitivity analysis to the campaign evaluator. Current default is 5bps which is optimistic for momentum entries during breakouts. Deliverables: (1) slippage_sensitivity array on campaign config that re-runs each variant at multiple slippage levels (recommended: 5/15/30 bps) and reports the per-level performance degradation curve. (2) Turnover cost report for dynamic-universe campaigns: total universe-rotation trades per era, estimated friction at each slippage level, net-of-friction returns compared to fixed-list baselines. (3) A breakeven_slippage_bps field on each result row showing the slippage level at which the strategy's deployment-matched excess return goes to zero. This tells the operator exactly how much execution quality matters. No new runtime logic needed — the slippage_bps field already exists on ExperimentConfig. Implementation is a loop over existing infrastructure at multiple slippage levels. Additive sidecar, not governance-gating, per the same graduation policy as Q-083 metrics.
completed.prereq: Q-085 Tier 2 complete (dynamic universe results available for turnover cost comparison). Multi-era framework operational (done).
completed.close_note: Slippage sensitivity sweep complete. C-lite v2 breaks even at 24.6 bps vs Fixed Tech 12 at 9.9 bps. C-lite v2 is clearly more friction-tolerant. Two-sub-sleeve case strengthened.

completed.title: Broader universe campaign — test momentum logic beyond the frozen 6-name sleeve
completed.what: Run the regime_aware_momentum strategy logic against a broader universe (20-30 names, e.g. S&P 100 subset or sector leaders) to determine whether the edge is specific to the frozen 6-name sleeve or generalizes to a wider pool. Two sub-questions: (1) does the same entry/exit logic produce comparable risk-adjusted returns on a broader universe? (2) does a dynamic screening rule (top N by trailing momentum from a larger pool, refreshed monthly) outperform the fixed 6-name list? Use the multi-era framework (Q-083) to test across all 5 eras. This directly addresses concentration risk and survivorship bias in the current frozen winner. Ownership: Claude proposes candidate universe lists + screening rules. Codex runs the campaigns. Both review results.
completed.prereq: Q-083 Phase 1 multi-era framework operational (done). Frozen winner multi-era baseline results available for comparison (done, run 2026-04-13).
completed.close_note: Broader universe research complete. Tier 1 (4 fixed universes), Tier 2 (dynamic trailing-return), C-lite v1+ablation+v2 all run. Edge is tech momentum. C-lite v2 (0.8707 STRONG) is the recommended dynamic screen. Two-sub-sleeve architecture validated.

completed.title: Multi-era validation extension + cross-era consistency scoring + trial ledger
completed.what: Extend the validation campaign runner to support named era windows, per-era reporting, cross-era consistency scoring, equity-curve structural break diagnostics, and a trial ledger sidecar for future DSR/PBO overfitting quantification. Four phased deliverables: (1) multi-era campaign config extension with named era windows and per-era + cross-era reports, (2) equity-curve break diagnostics via PELT on strategy equity curves per era and overall, (3) trial ledger + sidecar DSR/PBO computation as research-side metrics (additive, not governance-gating), (4) regime-conditional parameter sweeps only AFTER the single-parameter baseline is proven across eras. Guardrails: no live trading path changes, no relaxing anti-lookahead or reproducibility rules, no retroactive era cherry-picking after results are seen, no PASS/FAIL policy changes until new metrics are calibrated, all new metrics start as additive sidecars before governance. Ownership split: Claude specs config shape + era catalog + report fields + research framing + reviews implementation. Codex owns typed contract + evaluator integration + backward compatibility + guardrails.
completed.prereq: Q-076B manual campaigns complete (frozen winner exists). Validation runtime generalized (Q-076A). Spec doc from Claude delivered to Codex.
completed.close_note: Multi-era validation framework operational. 5-era catalog, consistency scoring, consumed-tape exclusion, universe coverage all live. Used across Q-085 research track.

completed.title: 🚨 Prove One Edge — walk-forward backtest of single signal family before rebuild commit
completed.what: Parallel track to Q-068/069/070. From Codex audit action #10: 'Reframe the project for the next month as prove one edge rather than improve the platform.' Pick ONE signal family. Strip the indicator stack — bare signal only, no HMM, vol persistence, jump var, tail dep, conviction gates, or other layers. Build minimal walk-forward backtest harness over 2-3 years across bull/bear/choppy regimes. Report standalone expectancy, Sharpe, max DD, regime sensitivity. Decision gate: yes → rebuild has a target worth aiming at, proceed with Q-070. No → reframe project as decision-support not full automation, scope down rebuild accordingly. Codex verdict: 'system looks more like an ambitious research/automation platform than a demonstrated alpha engine.' Platform work and strategy validation are different projects we've been treating as one. Splitting them is the highest-leverage move available.
completed.prereq: Codex rebuild blueprint will recommend which signal family to backtest first. Concurrent with Q-068, blocks Q-070 commit.

completed.title: 🚨 Full system audit — canonical/workspace drift, contract mismatches, dead gates
completed.what: Read-only catalog of every silent failure in the pipeline. Per-agent prompt source matrix (canonical vs workspace, all 16 agents). End-to-end JSON contract field name matrix (every producer/consumer pair). Gate/filter trace: live, dead, or contradicted upstream? Indicator backtest audit (HMM, vol persistence, jump var, tail dep — backtested in isolation? standalone edge?). LLM cost vs deterministic alternatives. Dead code catalog. CRITICAL: read-only, no fixes during audit phase. Triggered by 2026-04-07 discovery of conviction gate silently broken since 2026-03-26 (field name mismatch + upstream filter neuter).
completed.prereq: None. Codex audit running in parallel — findings to be merged.

completed.title: 🚨 Architectural proposal — simplified target + Pydantic contracts + single source of truth
completed.what: Based on Q-068 + Codex findings, propose simplified target architecture. Likely 4-6 agents (down from 16). Pydantic schemas for every JSON contract — schema is the contract, write-time validation. Single source of truth policy for prompts (canonical OR workspace, never both, with sync verification). Integration test framework with synthetic fixtures and end-to-end chains. Migration plan from current to target. Pressure-tested by OpenClaw + Codex + Claude before commitment.

completed.title: Telegram approval transport — Decision Support short-term channel
completed.what: Wire OpenClaw Telegram gateway as the first approval interface for Decision Support mode. Reuse the existing Discord server for broadcast and incident fan-out rather than rebuilding that layer. (1) Notification template: when a live-ready plan exists, OpenClaw sends symbols, side, sizing/risk, brief rationale, and expiry timer to Jacob via Telegram DM. (2) Reply parsing: approve, reject, reduce-size mapped to doc-16 approval states (PENDING -> APPROVED/REJECTED/EXPIRED). (3) Decision recording: write to approval_queue.json (status update) and approval_events.jsonl (immutable audit trail). (4) Timeout/expiry: unanswered PENDING items auto-expire after N minutes. (5) Dry-run validation: one full cycle with fake plan data before any real capital touches this path.
completed.prereq: OpenClaw gateway running. approval_queue.json and approval_events.jsonl schemas finalized (doc 16). Rebuild core producing live-ready plans.

completed.title: Generalize validation runtime beyond Q-072 + doc 10 metrics
completed.what:
The validation runtime is still Q-072-shaped: hardcoded to one strategy family, one catalyst type, one side (LONG), and a <=3 day hold limit. The data provider only fetches earnings catalysts. The evaluator computes raw benchmark comparison without Sharpe, Sortino, or Calmar. Half the Q-076B strategy candidates (sector rotation, correlation breakout, longer-hold post-earnings drift, mean-reversion) literally cannot be expressed in the current runtime.

This slice generalizes the foundation:
1. Strategy registry — each strategy is a module at src/openclaw_core/strategies/<name>.py implementing init() + evaluate() → list[StrategySignal]. The CLI discovers strategies by name, not hardcoded import.
2. Flexible signal contract — support LONG and SHORT, variable hold periods (1-60 days), multiple entry signal types (catalyst, momentum, mean-reversion, regime-change).
3. Data provider generalization — OHLCV is universal, but catalyst data should be pluggable (earnings calendar, sector strength, correlation matrix, regime state). Each strategy declares what data it needs.
4. Doc 10 metric additions to the evaluator:
   - Deployment-matched benchmark (SPY only on days strategy was active)
   - active_day_exposure_pct, average_capital_deployed_pct
   - Risk-adjusted metrics: annualized return, volatility, Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar
   - Sample-quality gates: min trade count, profitable-fold threshold, expectancy-decay check
5. Verdict governance — PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE only, benchmark rationale required in experiment config, promotion policy separate from results.
6. Experiment config expanded — benchmark_rationale field, strategy_params flexible typing, max_hold_days uncapped (strategy declares its own), time_window presets (full, COVID, bear, bull).

EXIT CRITERIA: A new strategy module (not catalyst_followthrough) can be defined, run through the CLI, and produce a verdict with deployment-matched benchmark and risk-adjusted metrics. The Q-072 artifacts must still work unchanged (backward compat).
completed.prereq: None — this is pure infrastructure unblocking everything downstream.

completed.title: First 2-3 strategy skeletons — manual backtest campaigns
completed.what:
With Q-076A complete, define and run the first 2-3 strategy skeletons through the generalized runtime. Start with the candidates most likely to produce signal and most aligned with existing infrastructure:

Recommended first batch (pick 2-3, not all):
1. Regime-aware momentum — buy when HMM=CALM + price above N-day MA + catalyst. Leverages HMM (Q-028), no-trade zone (Q-030), VIX contrarian flag (Q-031). Most infrastructure already exists.
2. Post-earnings drift — the documented multi-week anomaly (NOT Q-072's 1-3 day follow-through). 10-20 day hold, enter on earnings beat, wider universe. Different thesis than Q-072.
3. Defensive regime filter value test — compare 'always in market' vs 'regime-gated entry' using the HMM. This tests whether the regime layer ITSELF earns its keep, which informs every other strategy.

Each skeleton:
- Gets a standalone strategy module implementing the registry interface from Q-076A
- Gets an experiment config with explicit benchmark rationale
- Runs across multiple time windows (2018-2026 full, 2020-2022 COVID/recovery, 2022-2023 bear, 2023-2026 bull)
- Produces a verdict with doc 10 honest metrics
- Codex runs manually with mid-campaign course correction (bucket splits, parameter tightening)

If a skeleton PASSES: it becomes the deterministic base the LLM thesis engine wraps. Pipeline becomes: backtested skeleton decides WHEN → LLM adds conviction → portfolio builder gates risk → execution.
If all FAIL: that's real information. Expand the candidate list from the remaining Q-076 hypotheses (mean-reversion, VIX contrarian, sector rotation, quality-gated momentum, correlation breakout, breakout on volume).

IMPORTANT: Each campaign must use doc 10 governance. Deployment-matched benchmark. Risk-adjusted metrics. Sample gates. No benchmark framing shortcuts.
completed.blocker: Q-076A must be complete (generalized runtime + doc 10 metrics)
completed.prereq: Q-076A complete. Strategy registry working. At least one non-Q-072 strategy module can run through the CLI.

completed.title: Execution bridge — route rebuild plans through legacy executor
completed.what: Build a thin adapter that bridges rebuild trade plans to the proven legacy executor (bin/order_submitter.py), NOT a new execution engine. The legacy executor has 3+ weeks of real Alpaca paper fills, 6+ execution gates (staleness, conviction, stub defense, no-trade zones, circuit breaker, approval state), position sizing, order reconciliation, and a hard live-capital block. Rebuilding this from scratch would be premature and risky. Adapter flow: (1) read operator_mode_state.json to confirm execution_enabled, (2) read trade_plan.json + approval queue status, (3) translate rebuild TradePlan into legacy strategy_spec.json format, (4) call bin/order_submitter.py, (5) parse execution_log.json and mirror into rebuild execution_report.json. Long-term goal: extract the legacy executor into a modular, reusable block that both rebuild and any future pipeline can call. But phase 1 is bridge-only — no reimplementation of broker plumbing.
completed.blocker: Q-073 must be proven first (at least one successful dry-run cycle)
completed.prereq: Q-073 Telegram approval transport working (at least one dry-run cycle). Legacy executor verified for paper orders. Rebuild trade_plan.json producing valid plans.

closed_archive.title: YouTube video production pipeline — ElevenLabs → video → captions → thumbnail → upload
closed_archive.what:
Automated production pipeline that takes a finished script (from Q-016) and outputs a published YouTube video. Human input is already done at this stage — this is pure production automation.

STAGE 1 — VOICE: ElevenLabs Professional Voice Clone. Jacob records 30+ min of clean audio (reading any natural text — doesn't need to be scripted). Submit via ElevenLabs API to create a persistent voice model. All future voiceover generation uses this model — one-time setup cost, infinite reuse. API key already configured in OpenClaw. Output: MP3 voiceover file per script segment.

STAGE 2 — VISUALS: Three-layer composition.
(a) Screen recordings: auto-capture from terminal sessions using asciinema or OBS scripted capture. Triggered during actual pipeline runs — real terminal output, real state files, real code. Not staged.
(b) Architecture diagrams: generate Mermaid diagrams from the pipeline's agent topology, animate transitions with ffmpeg. These are accurate because they're generated from the actual prompt files and cron jobs.
(c) B-roll transitions: AI video generation (Runway ML, Pika, or similar) for abstract visual segments between technical sections. Keep to <20% of total runtime — the real footage is the differentiator.

STAGE 3 — ASSEMBLY: ffmpeg pipeline stitches audio + screen recordings + b-roll per script timing cues. Script markdown format includes explicit segment markers: [SCREEN:filename], [BROLL:prompt], [DIAGRAM:agent-topology]. Agent reads markers and assembles accordingly.

STAGE 4 — CAPTIONS: OpenAI Whisper transcribes final audio and generates .srt file. Burn captions into video with ffmpeg. Style: clean sans-serif, high contrast, lower-third placement. Not Comic Sans. Not rainbow colors. Minimal and professional.

STAGE 5 — THUMBNAIL: AI image generation (DALL-E or Stable Diffusion via API) using a consistent brand template. Template elements: dark background, bold title text overlay, small brand mark. Batch generate 3 variants per video, pick best. Consistent visual identity across all thumbnails is more important than any individual thumbnail being perfect.

STAGE 6 — UPLOAD: YouTube Data API v3. Upload video, set title/description/tags from script metadata. Description template: hook paragraph, timestamps (auto-generated from script segment markers), GitHub repo link, Ko-fi/donation link, affiliate links (Alpaca, ElevenLabs, Anthropic API). Schedule publish time rather than immediate upload — Tuesday/Thursday 9am ET performs well for technical audiences.

Phased build: Stage 1 (voice clone) + Stage 6 (upload) first. Then Stage 4 (captions). Then Stage 5 (thumbnail). Stages 2+3 (visuals) last — start with screen recordings only before adding AI b-roll.

Monetization hooks baked into every video: GitHub repo link (builds stars/credibility), Ko-fi link ($5 prompt pack access), affiliate links. These go in the description template from day one.
closed_archive.prereq: Q-016 content trigger agent producing well-structured scripts. ElevenLabs Professional Voice Clone recorded (30+ min audio). YouTube channel created. YouTube Data API v3 credentials. Ko-fi or Gumroad account for donation/paid access link.

closed_archive.title: Trading system isolation — dedicated agent + on/off switch
closed_archive.what:
Move all 18 trading cron jobs from the main agent to a new dedicated trading agent with its own isolated workspace. Goal: pause or resume the entire trading system as a unit instead of toggling 18 jobs individually.

Current state: all trading cron jobs run under main. OpenClaw has no native agent start/stop (openclaw agents supports add/delete/bind only). Cron disable is ID-by-ID only — no bulk or agent-scoped toggle.

Proposed design:
1. Create trading agent in openclaw.json with dedicated workspace (~/.openclaw/agents/trading/workspace)
2. Re-point all 18 TradingBot cron jobs to agentId: trading
3. Migrate relevant state files to new workspace
4. Build trading-pause.sh / trading-resume.sh shell scripts that bulk-disable/enable all trading job IDs via openclaw cron disable <id> — since no native group toggle exists, script the IDs
5. Optionally: wire pause/resume to a single gate file (trading_active.json) checked by the pre-market agent — more elegant but step 4 works immediately

Jobs to migrate (18 total): Preflight 6am goals, Morning brief curator, trading:premarket-phase, Morning brief composer, trading:preopen-gateway, TradingBot Market Status, Universe, Research, Alt Sentiment, Thesis, Strategy, Validation, Broker Auth, Risk Decision, Systems Audit, Remediation, Risk Approval, Pre-Open Refresh, Execution, Positions (x3), Volatility (x2), EOD Decision, Post-Close, Daily Audit, Governance Review.

Backup: unchanged — prompt files stay in OpenClaw-s-Brain on GitHub.

Prereq check needed: confirm openclaw cron edit supports changing agentId on existing jobs, or whether jobs must be deleted and re-added.
closed_archive.prereq: Confirm openclaw cron edit supports agentId changes on existing jobs.

closed_archive.title: Tax & accounting setup checklist
closed_archive.what: Set up the financial infrastructure to support the LLC properly and protect deductions. Key steps: (1) Open dedicated business checking — never mix personal and business money, this is non-negotiable. (2) Set up expense tracking — Wave (free) or QuickBooks Simple Start. Save every API invoice, hardware receipt, and subscription confirmation to a dedicated folder. (3) Understand Schedule C — single-member LLC files taxes on Schedule C attached to personal 1040, no separate business return required. Florida has no state income tax so no state business return either. (4) Document pursuit of profit — keep a simple log of hours worked, goals, and business activities. Protects against IRS hobby classification. (5) Section 179 for hardware — new machine can be fully expensed in year one rather than depreciated. (6) Find a CPA before first tax filing — not before forming the LLC, but before filing. Early year losses (pre-revenue) can be carried forward to offset future income, but only if IRS agrees it's a business. CPA helps document intent. Deductible categories: LLM API subscriptions, hardware, software tools, internet (business %), home office (dedicated space %), education, domain/hosting.
closed_archive.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Dedicated business bank account opened.

closed_archive.title: Systems tab — strategy toggles, performance reports, parameter history
closed_archive.what: New first-class tab in the Vercel trading app. Strategy on/off toggles (equity execution, options wheel, options BPS) with confirmation UX. Weekly BPS performance report viewer (rendered from bps_performance_report_YYYY-MM-DD.md). Parameter change history timeline. Sector/symbol win rate heatmap. Tunables tab may merge into this. Premium design consistent with art-direction pass. Build after BPS has 1-2 weeks of real data.
closed_archive.blocker: BPS module needs 1-2 weeks of data first
closed_archive.prereq: BPS module running in production (crons active, weekly reports generating)

closed_archive.title: Reflection memory — narrative-indexed decision history with P&L feedback
closed_archive.what: Store per-symbol per-day reflection records that capture the full decision context: thesis narrative, alt-sentiment snapshot (crowd mood, urgency signals), key technicals at decision time (RSI, relative strength), the decision made + conviction level, and outcome (P&L at +1/3/5 days). Index on narrative regime, not just numbers — BM25 text search on narrative fields naturally surfaces emotional regime matches (FOMO squeeze, fear-driven oversell, earnings euphoria). At thesis time, Thesis agent retrieves 3 most similar past situations and their outcomes. One-line lesson-learned field generated after outcome is known. Records built from artifacts already produced (research_packet, alt_sentiment_packet, thesis_packet, execution_log, trade_journal). No new data sources needed — just persistence of what's currently consumed and discarded.
closed_archive.blocker: Need 1-2 weeks of paper trading data to populate reflection records. No value without historical decisions to retrieve.
closed_archive.prereq: Pipeline executing trades daily. trade_journal.py producing P&L data. Research v2 + Alt-Sentiment producing rich narrative data. BM25 library (rank_bm25 pip package).

closed_archive.title: Profit path — trading system product
closed_archive.what: Offer a managed paper→live trading service, signal subscription, or white-labeled pipeline to other traders. Highest upside but significant regulatory surface — must research RIA registration requirements, determine whether signal distribution constitutes investment advice under SEC rules, and draft appropriate disclaimers. Do not build or market this before: (a) 60-day paper gate passed with clean results, (b) LLC formed (Q-007), (c) legal review completed. Strategy: start as a signal transparency product (show the thesis, not a buy recommendation), build track record publicly, consult a securities attorney before any paid offering. Fund with Q-008 or Q-009 first.
closed_archive.blocker: 60-day paper gate not yet passed. Legal review required before any public offering.
closed_archive.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Clean paper trading track record. Securities attorney consultation. RIA registration research.

closed_archive.title: Pipeline Architecture & Strategy Guide
closed_archive.what: Comprehensive 30-40 page technical document covering the entire trading pipeline: signal generation, trade selection, quality scoring, risk management, options strategy, infrastructure, and research paper lineage. Written for Jacob + financial advisors. Lives in Obsidian vault (interlinked) + PDF export. Includes quick reference card.
closed_archive.prereq: None — writing project, not code.

closed_archive.title: Obsidian vault integration + daily performance reports
closed_archive.what: Set up Obsidian vault structure for trading knowledge graph. Daily post-close briefing notes (auto-generated) linking to symbols, agents, papers. Email delivery via SendGrid. Same content in Obsidian + email — evaluate which Jacob prefers.
closed_archive.prereq: Q-053 architecture doc (provides the template and structure).

closed_archive.title: LSTM Regime Classifier (R&D)
closed_archive.what: Upgrade from HMM (Markovian, memoryless) to LSTM-based regime detection capturing path dependency. The fRNN insight from Paper #4: decisions should consider the full price PATH, not just current state. Only begin after HMM has 30+ days of data to benchmark against. v1: train LSTM on same data, compare accuracy. v2: replace HMM if meaningfully better.
closed_archive.blocker: Need 30+ days of HMM regime data to benchmark against.
closed_archive.prereq: Q-028 (HMM) running 30+ days. PyTorch or TensorFlow for LSTM training.

closed_archive.title: Intraday execute-if-approved mode (Option C)
closed_archive.what:
Extend the late-session paper validation chain to include an execution step after approval. Today's late-session run proved the validation → approval path works (AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER, 2026-03-09). The missing piece is the execution leg.

Specific gap: Agent-12 reads preopen_refresh.json for execution_allowed. That file is not written in the current late-session chain, so Agent-12 fails on MISSING_ARTIFACT before it can place any orders.

Fix: add Step 7.5 to late_session_paper_validation_v1.md — write a minimal late-session preopen_refresh.json with execution_allowed=true only when approval_state=AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER and circuit_breaker_state != HALT. Then run Agent-12 against it. Agent-12 reads risk_decision.json (approved names + sizes), broker_auth_ok.json, and risk_policy.json — all of which are already written by the existing chain.

End-to-end flow when complete:
1. Manual trigger (Telegram command or openclaw cron --at)
2. Fresh scan → thesis → strategy (basket correlation) → validation (WEAK_EDGE gate) → risk eval → approval gate
3. If AUTO_APPROVED_PAPER: write late-session preopen_refresh.json → Agent-12 → paper orders placed
4. Write execution_log.json with picked names, order IDs, fill status
5. Summary posted to channel: names, approval state, order outcome

Constraints to bake in:
- max 1-2 names (hard cap, already in chain)
- minimum session time remaining: skip execution if < 30 min to close (no point entering)
- QUARTER sizing mandatory (REDUCE_ONLY inherited)
- paper only (live_trading_enabled=false gate)
- no order submission if market_status shows circuit_breaker_active=true

Test plan: run on a day where market is open, trigger manually at ~11am or 2pm, verify execution_log.json and broker paper order both appear. Do not test near close.
closed_archive.prereq: late_session_paper_validation_v1.md exists (done 2026-03-09). Approval path proven (done 2026-03-09). Agent-12 prompt already handles paper execution path.

closed_archive.title: Hedge-vs-Rebalance Decision Framework
closed_archive.what: When pipeline suggests reducing a position, compare cost-to-sell vs cost-to-hedge-with-puts. High alpha + cheap puts = hedge instead of sell. Low alpha or expensive puts = sell. Implements Paper #5's no-trade zone concept for sell decisions. v1: simple cost comparison in morning_position_reviewer.py. v2: full model with transaction costs, market impact, time decay, asymmetric shock response.
closed_archive.prereq: Q-030 (no-trade zone), protective_put_screener single-symbol mode.

closed_archive.title: HMM threshold empirical sweep
closed_archive.what: Current 35/65 thresholds set by judgment. After 60+ days of HMM output, sweep via Hall & Pagan simulation to find optimal values.
closed_archive.prereq: 60+ days of hmm_regime output (~late May 2026)

closed_archive.title: GSADF Bubble Detection — explosive price early warning
closed_archive.what: Generalized Supremum ADF test on SPY, QQQ, and held sector ETFs. Detects explosive price behavior (bubbles) before they burst. When bubble detected, proactively tighten circuit breaker thresholds. Weekly diagnostic. v1: GSADF test via arch package. v2: combine with EXCF, track contagion lag, auto-tighten circuit breaker.
closed_archive.prereq: arch Python package or custom GSADF implementation.

closed_archive.title: Form LLC
closed_archive.what: Form a single-member LLC to operate all business activities under. Unlocks deductibility of LLM subscriptions (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), hardware (new machine, Section 179), software tools, internet, home office, and education. You do not need revenue to form — you need to be operating in pursuit of profit, which you are. File with Florida Secretary of State, get an EIN from the IRS (free, online, 10 min), open a dedicated business checking account. Total cost: ~$125 filing + $138.75/year annual report.
closed_archive.prereq: Florida: file at sunbiz.org ($125 filing fee, $138.75/year annual report). No state income tax — LLC profits pass through to personal return only. Get EIN at IRS.gov (free, 10 min). Open dedicated business checking account after EIN is issued. Consult a CPA before first tax filing.

closed_archive.title: Deflated Sharpe Ratio for performance evaluation
closed_archive.what: Apply DSR correction (Bailey & Lopez De Prado, 2014) when evaluating strategy performance. Corrects for multiple testing, non-normal returns, data snooping. Probability that observed Sharpe is genuine. Flag when DSR < 0.5. v1: add to performance_aggregator.py. v2: per-strategy DSR for backtester (Q-025), walk-forward DSR, dashboard comparison chart.
closed_archive.prereq: performance_aggregator.py producing Sharpe. Enough trading days for meaningful statistics.

closed_archive.title: Cross-Market Contagion Monitor
closed_archive.what: ARDL-style monitoring of cross-index correlation dynamics. Track whether shocks to S&P 500, Nasdaq, international indices propagate to our sectors with measurable lag. When contagion risk elevated, flag for defensive posture. Extends sector_correlation_monitor.py. v1: add SPY/QQQ/EFA/EEM to correlation matrix with lagged correlations. v2: full ARDL model, regime-conditional contagion.
closed_archive.prereq: Q-029 (sector correlation monitor) as base.

closed_archive.title: Bull/Bear adversarial debate in Thesis agent
closed_archive.what: Add a two-pass adversarial debate pattern to Agent-06 (Thesis). After forming the initial thesis, run a second pass that argues against it — forces explicit bear case argumentation before finalizing conviction. If the bear case is stronger, downgrade conviction. Inspired by TauricResearch/TradingAgents bull/bear researcher pattern. Implementation: two-pass prompt within existing Agent-06 (not a new agent). Pass 1 forms thesis as today. Pass 2 receives the thesis and constructs the strongest bear case. Final output includes both arguments and adjusted conviction. Reduces confirmation bias in stock selection.
closed_archive.blocker: Need 1+ week of paper trading data to compare thesis quality before/after.
closed_archive.prereq: Research v2 stable. Pipeline producing daily thesis_packet.json with real data. Validate with pipeline-validate.sh before deploy.

closed_archive.title: Asymmetric tail dependence / regime-switching copula
closed_archive.what: Downside co-movement is stronger than upside across assets. Upgrade hedge sizing to be asymmetric (heavier puts in high-dependence regime). Future upgrade for Q-029 correlation monitor and protective put screener.
closed_archive.prereq: Q-028 HMM + Q-029 correlation monitor stable.

closed_archive.title: AI automation consulting / agency
closed_archive.what: Sell the skillset, not a product. You know how to build multi-agent systems with real infrastructure — most businesses don't have anyone who can do this. One retainer client at $3-5k/month covers all subscriptions and hardware. Services: custom agent pipelines, OpenClaw-style automation setups, AI workflow integration for small businesses. No product needed to start — just a landing page, a LinkedIn presence, and one warm outreach campaign. Strategy: close 1-2 clients to fund the hobby side, keep scope tight, productize repeatable work into templates over time.
closed_archive.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Simple contract template. Define service scope and pricing before first outreach.

closed_archive.title: Good Friday incident remediation (18 items)
closed_archive.what: Full sweep: BPS calendar gates, market calendar on all independent crons, Discord migration complete (all delivery configs), dependency-scoped skip propagation with typed reasons, validator timeout thresholds, heartbeat holiday awareness, morning brief cold-lane fix, watchdog removal. 0 items remaining.

closed_archive.title: CB auto-downstep in nightly validator
closed_archive.what: HALT→REDUCE_ONLY after 1 clean validation. REDUCE_ONLY→NORMAL after 3 consecutive clean nights. Counter resets on failure. All transitions reported to Discord #pipeline. Runs at 23:00 ET nightly only.

closed_archive.title: Discord server rebuild — primary ops notification hub
closed_archive.what: 6 new channels (#trades, #pipeline, #signals, #daily-recap, #build-log, #incidents). All pipeline agents wired to Discord. Telegram kept for morning brief + chat + critical dual-output. Old channels preserved as safety net.

closed_archive.title: Weekly directional options screener (5-7 DTE)
closed_archive.what: 8-gate directional options: CB NORMAL, quality >= 7, conviction > 65%, Hurst < 0.7, DTE 5-7, no earnings, OI > 500, cost 1-2% per position / 5% total. Separate path from protective puts. Writes weekly_directional_strategy.json. Wired into regime router.

closed_archive.title: Backtesting framework infrastructure
closed_archive.what: Walk-forward backtesting engine: prepare.py (data download + walk-forward windows), backtest.py (strategy execution with stops/targets/position limits), strategy module interface. 502 days, 12 symbols, 17 folds. Momentum baseline: 60.5% WR, 1.85 PF. Ready for strategy-specific backtests.

closed_archive.title: Strategy REDUCE_ONLY filter + --force-from orchestrator flag
closed_archive.what: Agent-06 skips new longs not in current portfolio during REDUCE_ONLY. Orchestrator --force-from clears failed artifact and resumes in one command.

closed_archive.title: Robust tail dependence estimator (Gumbel copula)
closed_archive.what: Dual-estimator tail dependence: MLE in calm regimes, Hellinger in volatile (2x variance but stable through crises). Computes chi for all sector ETF pairs. Enriches sector_correlations.json. Current: SPY/XLK chi=0.77 (77% joint extreme probability). Cron 07:10 ET Monday. Paper: Robust Tail Dependence (Ardakani 2025).

closed_archive.title: Protective put sizing formula fix + idempotency guard
closed_archive.what: Fixed contracts_needed from qty to qty//100. Added date-match idempotency check to options_regime_router.py. BX 165-contract overhedge traced to this bug.

closed_archive.title: Paper-mode governance tolerance
closed_archive.what: Agent-08b severity tier system (SAFETY/GOVERNANCE/CARRYOVER) with paper vs live mode behavior. GOVERNANCE checks warn+continue in paper, hard block in live. Implemented across v1.1-v1.5 of Agent-08b prompt.

closed_archive.title: Options concentration gate + dashboard deployed capital
closed_archive.what: Max 50% options notional in single underlying (gate_options_risk.py check 5). Dashboard now shows equity_deployed + options_deployed split. Live API fixed to include options in positions_value.

closed_archive.title: JV/RV decomposition (jump vs regime volatility)
closed_archive.what: Bipower variation decomposes realized vol into jump component (event vol, decays fast) vs smooth diffusive component (regime vol, persists). Enriches market_status.json. Current reading: DIFFUSION_DOMINATED (no jump component, all regime vol). Phase 2 Pass 3 covered call signal.

closed_archive.title: HMM threshold calibration (asymmetric hysteresis)
closed_archive.what: Replaced Viterbi hard state assignment with probability-based classification using asymmetric hysteresis. VOLATILE entry: calm_prob < 0.35. CALM entry: calm_prob > 0.65. Hysteresis band (0.35-0.65) maintains previous regime to prevent whipsaw. Paper: Hall & Pagan (2025).

closed_archive.title: Change-point coincidence for correlation monitor
closed_archive.what: PELT-based structural break detection across 11 sector ETFs + SPY. Measures pairwise break coincidence — assets that crash together even with low daily correlation. Enriches sector_correlations.json. Current: SPY/XLC/XLY/XLK all broke simultaneously (100% coincidence). Cron 07:05 ET Monday after correlation monitor. Paper: Semi-Metric Portfolio (James et al. 2023).

closed_archive.title: Vol persistence indicator (Hurst exponent)
closed_archive.what: Rolling Hurst exponent on SPY realized volatility. Enriches market_status.json with vol_persistence field. H > 0.7 = persistent vol (dont fade), H < 0.3 = anti-persistent (safe to fade). Runs as orchestrator post-Market-Status enrichment alongside HMM. Foundation for Phase 2 covered call timing gate. Paper: Long-Memory SV (Chaim & Laurini 2024).

closed_archive.title: Per-agent model routing + openai-codex retirement
closed_archive.what: Retired openai-codex (expired OAuth, unsanctioned endpoint). Implemented per-agent model routing: 28 Haiku (gates/rules), 8 Sonnet (reasoning), 3 Gemini Flash (data-heavy). Added kill_running_session() to orchestrator for timeout cleanup. Zombie reaper tightened 30m→15m. Routing policy upgraded to v3. Gemini 2.5 Flash live and verified.

closed_archive.title: Per-Trade Quality Score — 5-dimension assessment (D/M/R/P/I)
closed_archive.what: Added quality scoring to Agent-07 (Validation v1.6): Data freshness, Model confidence, Robustness, Risk/Reward, Interpretability. Each 0-2, max 10. Quality gate: total < 5 = FAIL. Agent-08b v1.4 check #8 enforces the gate. Paper #7 inspired.

closed_archive.title: CVaR Risk Metric — tail-risk aware sizing
closed_archive.what: Historical CVaR calculator (95% and 99%) from portfolio equity series. Runs post-close 16:02 ET weekdays. Outputs cvar_metrics.json with tail-risk estimates, budget ratios, and data confidence. Agent-08b v1.5 reads CVaR as informational context (CVAR_BUDGET_WARN when exposure > budget). 25 observations currently — confidence will improve over time.

closed_archive.title: YouTube content trigger agent — 5 bullets in, publish-ready script out
closed_archive.what:
The content layer of the YouTube pipeline. Automation handles production (Q-017) but content must be anchored in real human experience — this agent bridges the two. Jacob writes 5 bullet points in 5 minutes describing what happened, why it matters, and what was learned. The agent produces a full publish-ready video script.

Why this is not fully automated: AI-generated scripts from source code alone produce dry, generic, commodity content. The 5-bullet input from Jacob is the non-negotiable human layer that makes the content real. It preserves authenticity — the thing that actually separates this channel from the hundreds of AI trading bot channels that will never build an audience.

INPUT FORMAT (Jacob provides):
- What happened (1 sentence)
- Why it matters to the system (1 sentence)
- What was surprising or hard (1 sentence)
- What the fix or decision was (1 sentence)
- What a viewer should take away (1 sentence)
Optional: list of relevant source files or state files to include as context.

AGENT READS (automatically, no manual input):
- Relevant agent prompt files from OpenClaw-s-Brain (inferred from bullet content)
- Relevant state JSON files from trading-bot/state/ (for real data to reference)
- queue.json completed section (for accurate history/timeline)
- Pipeline architecture (agent list, stage sequence, models)

OUTPUT: Structured script markdown with:
- HOOK (0:00-0:30): opening that leads with the most interesting thing — the failure, the discovery, the result. Never start with 'in this video I will show you.'
- CONTEXT (0:30-2:00): brief setup, what the system is, why this session matters. Assume viewer knows Python/AI basics but not this specific system.
- CORE (2:00-8:00): the actual content. Walk through what happened with real references to code, state files, and architecture. Segment markers for screen recordings and diagrams: [SCREEN:filename], [DIAGRAM:topology], [BROLL:description].
- TAKEAWAY (8:00-9:30): what this means for the broader project, what comes next, honest assessment of current state.
- OUTRO + CTA (9:30-10:00): subscribe, GitHub link, Ko-fi link, next video tease.
- METADATA block: suggested title (3 variants), description (full, with timestamps auto-filled from segment markers), tags, thumbnail text suggestions.

SERIES TEMPLATES (agent picks the right one based on bullet content):
- POST-MORTEM: incident → root cause → fix → lesson. Best for governance failures, bugs, unexpected blocks. Highest retention because failure is interesting.
- BUILD LOG: here is what I added, why I added it, how it works. Best for new features (Agent-06 basket correlation, etc.).
- DAY-N RECAP: what the system picked today, how it performed, what the approval gate did. Recurring format, builds longitudinal audience investment.
- EXPLAINER: deep dive on one concept (multi-agent systems, risk approval gates, paper trading architecture). Evergreen content, high search volume.

QUALITY RULES baked into the agent prompt:
- Never open with 'in this video' — lead with the most interesting thing
- Write for someone who is smart but has never seen this codebase
- Reference real numbers, real file names, real decisions — not generic descriptions
- Honest about failures and limitations — this is the differentiator vs. polished demo channels
- Script should sound like Jacob talking, not like a Wikipedia article
- Target runtime: 8-12 minutes for explainers/post-mortems, 4-6 minutes for day-N recaps

Brand positioning this content builds toward: Jacob Barkley — developer building a real AI trading system in public, showing what actually works and what breaks. Not a trading tips channel. Not a generic AI tutorial channel. A transparent engineering journal with a financial edge.
closed_archive.prereq: YouTube channel created with clear series concept. A few real incidents/sessions documented to use as first video content (today's governance loop incident + the basket quality improvements are both strong first videos). Decide on channel name/identity before building the agent so brand voice can be baked into the prompt.
closed_archive.close_note: Closed — superseded by Q-018 (autonomous story agent)

closed_archive.title: YouTube / content + tools
closed_archive.what: Document the build publicly. 'I built a 16-agent AI trading pipeline' is genuinely interesting content with a real audience. Monetizes through AdSense, sponsorships, and naturally leads to selling templates, prompt packs, or the OpenClaw setup as a product. Lowest regulatory risk, lowest startup cost, builds audience before you have anything to sell — audience is the asset. Strategy: start with build-log style videos (what you built, why, what broke), layer in tutorials as the system matures, eventually gate advanced content or tools behind a paid tier.
closed_archive.prereq: LLC formed (Q-007). Basic recording setup. Decide on channel identity and branding.
closed_archive.close_note: Closed — superseded by Q-017 and Q-018 (specific YouTube items)

Status vocab enforcement across all prompts and Python scripts — no more EXECUTION_FAILED for gated non-execution, consistent SKIPPED_GATED and ERROR_INTERNAL. Integration test suite covering clean happy path, stale artifact contamination, invalid policy, upstream risk halt. See refactor plan Phase 5A-D.

closed_archive.title: Trading pipeline Phase 3 — 4-gate Python scripts + health simplification
closed_archive.what: Create bin/gate_session.py (validates session_context.json), bin/gate_artifacts.py (validates artifact_manifest.json freshness for active run), bin/gate_policy.py (validates normalized_risk_policy.json). Four canonical gates replace scattered check logic across agents. closed_archive.title: Stocktwits integration — finance-specific sentiment feed

Design the combined sentiment layer so Agent-04 can weight Reddit narrative (squeeze/macro), Stocktwits momentum (ticker-level), and ApeWisdom rank (retail crowd direction) as a unified retail sentiment score — not three independent signals. closed_archive.title: Persistent story agent — continuously generates YouTube scripts from the real journey
closed_archive.what:
A background agent that runs on a schedule (nightly or on git push to OpenClaw-s-Brain) and turns the real, ongoing story of building this system into a backlog of staged YouTube scripts. Jacob does not trigger this manually — it runs whether he thinks about content or not. By the time the recording setup is ready, there should be 30-50 scripts waiting.

SOURCE MATERIAL (what the agent reads):
- Git commits to OpenClaw-s-Brain (commit messages + diffs — this is the primary story log)
- queue.json completed[] section (milestone log — what shipped and when)
- trading-bot/state/ files (approvals, rejections, circuit breaker changes, new incidents)
- runbooks/ (decisions documented, go/no-go assessments)
- The running story-arc.md doc (see below) — what has been told, what is next
Do NOT read raw Claude Code session JSONL files — they contain API keys and sensitive data.

STORY ARC DOCUMENT (story-arc.md in the private repo):
The agent maintains a living document that tracks: where we are in the journey (days since first paper trade, paper gate progress, key milestones), what has already been scripted (prevents repetition), what narrative threads are open (e.g., 'governance loop introduced ep 3, fix shown ep 5, proof shown ep 7'), and what the next 3 most interesting things to cover are. Every script generation updates this document. This is what makes the scripts feel like a series instead of isolated videos.

SCRIPT OUTPUT FORMAT (one .md file per script):
```
---
episode: <N>
series: building-ai-trading-system
topic_tags: [governance, incident, agent-10]
status: DRAFT
generated_at: <ISO date>
story_arc_position: <where this fits in the narrative>
title_variants:
  - <option 1 — curiosity gap>
  - <option 2 — direct/technical>
  - <option 3 — result-first>
thumbnail_brief: <text overlay + emotional direction + visual concept>
description_seo: <first 150 chars must contain primary keyword, full description below>
tags: [comma, separated, youtube, tags]
chapters:
  - 0:00 Hook
  - 0:30 Context
  ...
community_question: <specific question to end the video with>
---

[HOOK]
... (first 30 seconds — most interesting thing first, never 'in this video')

[CONTEXT]
... (setup, what system is, why this session matters — assume smart viewer, no prior context)

[CORE]
... (real content with segment markers)
[SCREEN:filename] for terminal/code captures
[DIAGRAM:agent-topology] for architecture visuals
[CARD:topic] where a future video should be linked
[BROLL:description] for transition visuals
[PATTERN_INTERRUPT] every 2-3 min — flag where pace/visual should shift

[TAKEAWAY]
... (honest assessment, what comes next in the arc)

[OUTRO]
... (value-first CTA, cross-link to related episode, community question)
```

YOUTUBE STRATEGY RULES baked into the agent prompt (not vague instructions — explicit rules):
1. Hook rule: open with the most interesting single thing — the failure, the number, the discovery. Never open with context or setup.
2. Title rule: curiosity gap + specificity. 'The fake error message my AI generated about itself' > 'Bug fix video'. Numbers always help.
3. CTA rule: first subscribe ask comes AFTER the first real value delivery, not at the start. Viewers who get value first are 3x more likely to subscribe.
4. Chapter rule: every script gets timestamps auto-generated from section markers. YouTube actively pushes chaptered videos.
5. Cross-link rule: plant [CARD:topic] markers where related content should be referenced. Use natural language: 'I go much deeper on X in episode N — link in the description.' Card library builds as scripts accumulate.
6. Pattern interrupt rule: flag [PATTERN_INTERRUPT] every 2-3 minutes. Production team changes visual or cuts to screen recording to reset attention.
7. SEO rule: description first 150 chars contain primary keyword — that is what appears in search results. Write this line first.
8. Community rule: every video ends with one specific, answerable question. Not 'let me know what you think.' Something like: 'What would you have done differently when the remediation loop first fired on day 1?'
9. Series arc rule: every script references at least one prior episode and teases at least one future episode. Builds watch time across the channel.
10. Honesty rule: this is the differentiator. The script must reference real file names, real error codes, real decisions. Generic descriptions get cut. Vague language gets cut.

REPO STRUCTURE (private GitHub repo: youtube-content):
```
scripts/
  chronological/          # 2026-03-09-governance-loop-incident.md
  by-topic/
    trading-pipeline/
    incidents-post-mortems/
    architecture/
    build-log/
  series/
    building-ai-trading-system/  # episode sequence, numbered
assets/
  thumbnail-briefs/       # one per script
  diagram-specs/          # mermaid specs auto-generated
meta/
  story-arc.md            # living document, updated each run
  keyword-research.md     # YouTube search term targets, updated monthly
  content-calendar.md     # what is ready to produce vs. staged
```

RUN SCHEDULE: Nightly cron via OpenClaw (low priority, runs after midnight ET). Also triggered on git push to OpenClaw-s-Brain when commit message contains significant keywords (fix, incident, proof, complete, approved). Generates 1-3 scripts per run depending on how much happened. Never generates more than 3 per run — quality over volume.

BRAND VOICE embedded in agent prompt: Jacob Barkley — developer building a real AI trading system in public. Not a trading tips channel. Not a generic AI tutorial. A transparent engineering journal with a financial edge. Tone: direct, technically honest, occasionally self-deprecating about failures, never polished or corporate. The audience is developers and technically curious people, not retail traders looking for stock tips.

FIRST SCRIPTS TO GENERATE (day 1 backlog already exists from 2026-03-09):
1. Architecture explainer — the 16-agent pipeline, how it works, why it is built this way
2. Governance loop incident post-mortem — the fake error that blocked 3 days of paper trades
3. Basket quality fix — why XOM+CVX was a redundant bet and how we fixed the selector
4. The paper vs. live governance calibration problem — why the system was too safe for its own good
closed_archive.prereq: Private GitHub repo created (youtube-content). Channel name and brand identity decided — brand voice must be baked into agent prompt before first run. story-arc.md initialized with current journey state (day 1, paper trading, 16 agents live). Q-016 content trigger agent optional — this agent is the autonomous version of the same concept.
closed_archive.close_note: Closed — done, nightly story agent cron running at 02:00 ET

closed_archive.title: Harden options_regime contract for live options sleeve (Phase 2)
closed_archive.what: Agent-06 and Agent-07 currently default to IV_ELEVATED when options_regime is missing or invalid (TICKET-018 conservative default). When the options sleeve activates in Phase 2, this default must become a hard fail with code MISSING_CONTRACT_INPUT: options_regime. Update both prompts: remove the IV_ELEVATED fallback, enforce hard fail on missing/invalid field, and remove the 'options sleeve inactive' conditional comment. Do not make this change before the options sleeve is live — silent defaulting is safer than hard failing in paper-only mode.
closed_archive.blocker: Options sleeve not yet active. Do not harden until Phase 2 options trading is enabled.
closed_archive.prereq: 60-day paper gate passed. Options sleeve enabled in risk_policy.json. Agent-06 and Agent-07 prompts updated to v1.3+.
closed_archive.close_note: Closed — obsolete, BPS replaced wheel strategy entirely

Updated all three in ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json to use canonical paths (Agent-08-Risk-v1.1.md, Agent-09-Systems-Auditor-v1.1.md, Agent-10-Remediation-Manager-v1.1.md) and gpt-5.4. Agent-08b was already correct. Confirmed via openclaw cron list — all three now show gpt-5.4.

closed_archive.title: PostToolUse hook — dashboard auto-sync on file edits
closed_archive.what: Added PostToolUse hook to ~/.claude/settings.json that runs push-dashboard-data.sh after every Edit or Write tool call. Zero credit cost — pure bash, no LLM invocation. Also fixed push-dashboard-data.sh to stage queue.json alongside tickets.json so queue changes are never silently dropped. Root cause of today's missed Q-014/Q-015 push: queue.json was modified but the script only staged tickets.json.

closed_archive.what: Declared options_regime as explicit contract input in both prompts. Defined valid values (IV_LOW | IV_ELEVATED | IV_HIGH | IV_EXTREME). Conservative IV_ELEVATED default for Phase 1; hard fail path documented for Phase 2 options sleeve activation (tracked in Q-012).

closed_archive.title: Formalize Agent-12 live_trading_enabled contract input
closed_archive.what: Declared live_trading_enabled as explicit required contract input in Agent-12. Added MISSING_POLICY_FIELD fail code — no silent default. Added hard gate comment: true = live broker orders, false = paper only.

closed_archive.title: Fix encoding artifacts in all agent prompts
closed_archive.what: Cleaned double-encoded UTF-8 mojibake from all 14 affected agent prompts — em dashes, apostrophes, and emoji sequences all corrected. Zero artifacts remaining across all 17 prompt files.

closed_archive.title: Agent-09 v1.2 + Agent-10 v1.2 — governance loop fixes
closed_archive.what: Fixed two bugs that caused today's paper session to block on false positives. (1) Agent-09 was applying its own outbound messaging restrictions as a global audit policy against all upstream artifact alerts — risk_status_ping from Agent-06's strategy_spec alerts was being flagged as MSG_POLICY_VIOLATION (CRITICAL). Fixed by splitting Step 6 into an upstream alert allowlist (risk_status_ping explicitly authorized) and Agent-09's own outbound policy. (2) Agent-10 was not reading session_close.json, so criticals acknowledged at session close were carried forward as OPEN indefinitely. Fixed by adding session_close.json as optional input and resolving carry-forward items that match acknowledged_issues[]. Both prompt changes pushed to OpenClaw-s-Brain. State manually adjudicated: open_critical_count 2 → 0, circuit breaker HALT → REDUCE_ONLY.

closed_archive.title: Agent-06 v1.3 + Agent-07 v1.3 — basket quality improvements
closed_archive.what: Agent-06 v1.3: added primary_thesis_driver field per strategy item and STEP 3b basket correlation review. Items sharing the same primary_thesis_driver are de-duplicated — lower-conviction item excluded unless genuinely differentiated by sector, catalyst type, or hold horizon. Would have dropped CVX today (same OIL_REGIME_STRENGTH driver as XOM, no differentiated edge). Agent-07 v1.3: added 9th validation dimension edge_quality (STRONG | ACCEPTABLE | WEAK). WEAK_EDGE rule: thesis_alignment=PARTIAL AND warn_details >= 2 distinct concerns => rating auto-upgraded to FAIL. Would have failed GAP today (PARTIAL alignment + 2 distinct warns). Both changes pushed to OpenClaw-s-Brain commit 5bb4147.
